title: tithymalus azoricus
intro: Tithymalus azoricus, also known as Euphorbia azorica, is a plant species native to the Azores archipelago in the Atlantic Ocean. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Tithymalus azoricus are attributed to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions such as wounds and ulcers. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
